questions.question: Which statement best defines Clearance (CL)?
questions.option_b: Volume of plasma cleared of drug per unit time
questions.option_d: Constant fraction eliminated per unit time
questions.explanation: Higher clearance generally lowers steady-state concentration for a given dose rate.

questions.question: Which statement best defines Volume of distribution (Vd)?
questions.option_c: Volume of plasma cleared of drug per unit time
questions.option_d: Apparent volume relating amount in body to plasma concentration
questions.explanation: Large Vd suggests extensive tissue distribution.

questions.question: Which statement best defines Half-life (t1/2)?
questions.option_d: Time for plasma concentration to decrease by 50%
questions.explanation: t1/2 = 0.693·Vd/CL for first-order kinetics.

questions.question: Which statement best defines Steady state?
questions.option_a: Volume of plasma cleared of drug per unit time
questions.option_c: Occurs when rate of drug in equals rate of drug out
questions.explanation: Typically ~4–5 half-lives to reach ~95% steady state.

questions.question: Which statement best defines Loading dose?
questions.option_b: Occurs when rate of drug in equals rate of drug out
questions.option_c: Dose to promptly achieve target concentration
questions.explanation: LD = (Vd × Cp_target) / F.

questions.question: Which statement best defines Maintenance dose rate?
questions.option_a: Time for plasma concentration to decrease by 50%
questions.option_d: Dose rate to maintain target concentration
questions.explanation: MD_rate = (CL × Cp_target) / F.

questions.question: Which statement best defines First-order kinetics?
questions.option_a: Constant fraction eliminated per unit time
questions.option_d: Occurs when rate of drug in equals rate of drug out
questions.explanation: Half-life is constant; most drugs follow this.

questions.question: Which statement best defines Zero-order kinetics?
questions.option_d: Constant amount eliminated per unit time
questions.explanation: Half-life varies; seen with saturable elimination (e.g., ethanol).

questions.question: Which statement best defines Therapeutic drug monitoring (TDM)?
questions.option_c: Occurs when rate of drug in equals rate of drug out
questions.option_d: Constant fraction eliminated per unit time
questions.explanation: Useful for narrow therapeutic index drugs.

questions.question: Which statement best defines Bioavailability (F)?
questions.explanation: Affects required oral dose and loading/maintenance calculations.

questions.question: A drug has Vd = 60 L and clearance CL = 4 L/h (first-order). Approximately what is its half-life?
questions.explanation: Use t1/2 = 0.693·Vd/CL for first-order kinetics.

questions.explanation: Use t1/2 = 0.693·Vd/CL for first-order kinetics.

questions.explanation: Use t1/2 = 0.693·Vd/CL for first-order kinetics.

questions.question: For a drug with half-life of 4 hours (first-order), approximately how long to reach ~95% of steady state?
questions.explanation: Steady state is reached after ~4–5 half-lives (~95% by ~5 half-lives).

questions.question: For a drug with half-life of 6 hours (first-order), approximately how long to reach ~95% of steady state?
questions.explanation: Steady state is reached after ~4–5 half-lives (~95% by ~5 half-lives).

questions.question: For a drug with half-life of 8 hours (first-order), approximately how long to reach ~95% of steady state?
questions.explanation: Steady state is reached after ~4–5 half-lives (~95% by ~5 half-lives).

questions.explanation: Steady state is reached after ~4–5 half-lives (~95% by ~5 half-lives).

questions.question: For a drug with half-life of 16 hours (first-order), approximately how long to reach ~95% of steady state?
questions.explanation: Steady state is reached after ~4–5 half-lives (~95% by ~5 half-lives).

questions.question: (Clinical PK Item 42) Which parameter MOST directly determines maintenance dose rate at a target concentration?
questions.explanation: Maintenance dose rate is proportional to clearance at a given target concentration (MD_rate = CL×Cp/F).
